lynx.response:
Now I have a thorough understanding of the project. Here is my final onboarding analysis.

```json
{
  "architecture": [
    "DeepCodex is a macOS 14+ SwiftUI desktop app (10 Swift source files, 155 nodes, 523 edges) that serves as a graphical companion for managing OpenAI's Codex coding agent. It sits on top of a local shell-script toolchain at ~/Developer/ai-tools/codex-mode/.",
    "The app has three primary concerns: (1) mode switching between 'normal' (GPT-5.5), 'DeepSeek V4 Pro', and 'DeepSeek V4 Flash' model providers; (2) portable context export — reading Codex JSONL session files via embedded Python scripts and generating Markdown summaries to continue work across modes; (3) a completion alert system that polls a notifications/events directory and plays sounds when a task finishes, using Accessibility permissions to detect user attention.",
    "Architecture follows a classic SwiftUI single-window pattern: App.swift (@main) creates an AppState @StateObject, passes it to ContentView (the UI), and starts the menu-bar icon via MenuBarManager. All state mutation flows through AppState on the @MainActor. Shell execution is performed via Process (never async shell APIs) through static runShell/runCommand helpers.",
    "The project has no test targets, no test files, and zero automated test coverage. The single TESTS edge in the knowledge graph refers to a production function named testAlert (not an actual test)."
  ],
  "entry_points": [
    "DeepCodexApp (App.swift, line 5) — @main struct; creates AppState, renders ContentView, applies dark color scheme and hidden title bar.",
    "ContentView (ContentView.swift, line 14) — Root SwiftUI view; on appear calls MenuBarManager.shared.setup(with:) and renders header, action cards, tools panel, system info, and advanced capabilities.",
    "MenuBarManager.shared.setup() (MenuBarManager.swift, line 14) — Creates NSStatusItem in the menu bar showing current mode as a colored dot + label via an attributed title. Polls mode changes with a 2-second timer. Builds a dropdown menu for switching modes directly.",
    "CompletionAlertManager.startWatching() (CompletionAlertManager.swift, line 96) — Background event-loop; polls a notifications/events directory every second for new agent-turn-complete-*.json files, triggers sound alerts, and monitors mouse/keyboard activity to auto-dismiss.",
    "AppState.refreshStatus() (AppState.swift, line 168) — Called on first appear; runs status.sh, parses current-mode.json, checks Moon Bridge via lsof, kicks off advanced capabilities check, and starts alert watching."
  ],
  "important_modules": [
    "AppState (AppState.swift, 577 lines) — The brain of the app. @MainActor ObservableObject holding all published state (activeMode, moonBridgeStatus, detectedThreads, alert settings) and all action methods (switch modes, detect threads, export context, restore backup). Highest coupling in the system: 17 dependents on the class, 20 on the Mode enum. Key functions: runShell (fan-in 9), debugLog (fan-in 10), parseJson (complexity 8), detectLatestThreads (complexity 23, 104 lines).",
    "ContextExporter (ContextExporter.swift, 396 lines) — Pure read-only struct that is the most architecturally interesting module. Parses Codex JSONL session files by spawning python3 subprocesses with inline scripts. Provides 7 extraction methods (user messages, assistant messages, agent messages, function calls, file paths, errors, session meta) and a 126-line generateMarkdown() function that assembles a complete Markdown context document for migrating work between modes.",
    "CompletionAlertManager (CompletionAlertManager.swift, 340 lines) — Manages the alert lifecycle (inactive → sounding → attended) via directory polling, AVAudioPlayer/NSSound playback, and NSEvent global monitors for mouse/keyboard activity. Uses AXIsProcessTrusted for Accessibility permissions. Contains the shouldAlert(minimumTaskDuration) filter.",
    "ContentView (ContentView.swift, 874 lines) — God-component SwiftUI view with 15 subviews: headerSection, logoView, actionCards, advancedCapabilitiesSection, webSearchCapabilityCard, imageInputCapabilityCard, routingCapabilityCard, toolsPanel, systemInfoPanel. Handles all visual layout, hover states, sound selection menus, and screen-height auto-resizing via WindowSizer.",
    "ContinueWorkModal (ContinueWorkModal.swift, 675 lines) — Another god-component SwiftUI modal with 10 phase states (loading→empty→selecting→generating→ready→exporting→done→error). Groups threads by project folder (cwd), provides thread selection, destination mode picker, context preview, and the 'Copy and switch' action.",
    "AdvancedCapabilities (AdvancedCapabilities.swift, 163 lines) — Pure model layer with 6 Codable structs (AdvancedCapabilities, V1ModelsInfo, CodexWebSearchInfo, MoonBridgeWebSearchInfo, ImageInputInfo, RoutingInfo) and display helpers. Decodes JSON from advanced-capabilities-status.sh output."
  ],
  "hotspots": [
    "ContentView (874 lines, GOD_COMPONENT) — Largest file. Combines UI layout, state rendering, hover effects, and imperative window-resizing logic. Should be split into feature-specific sub-views.",
    "ContinueWorkModal (675 lines, GOD_COMPONENT) — Second largest. Encapsulates an entire wizard flow in one view. The 10-phase state machine and thread grouping logic could be extracted into a separate ViewModel.",
    "AppState (577 lines, GOD_COMPONENT) — Central coupling hub with 17 direct dependents. Contains UI display logic (modeTitle, modeSubtitle, modeColor computed properties), shell execution, file I/O, JSON parsing, and clipboard operations. Violates single-responsibility principle.",
    "detectLatestThreads (complexity 23, 104 lines) — Most complex function. Runs SQLite queries against ~/.codex/state_5.sqlite with two fallback strategies (JSON parsing → filesystem find+grep). Contains raw SQL string interpolation (SQL injection risk from provider string).",
    "parseJson (complexity 8, fan-in 7) — Parses current-mode.json with force-unwrapped optionals (guard let). No error recovery beyond silent return.",
    "ContextExporter.generateMarkdown (126 lines, GOD_COMPONENT) — Large string-building function that assembles Markdown with conditional sections, truncation logic, and date formatting.",
    "Shell.run in MenuBarManager (zsh -c pattern, fan-in 12) — Constructs shell commands via string concatenation rather than argument arrays, creating injection risk from mode names.",
    "checkMoonBridgeDirect (runs lsof via runCommand, fan-in 5) — Executes /usr/sbin/lsof with hardcoded TCP port 38440; no timeout on the process.",
    "No test coverage on any shell execution, JSON parsing, Python-script output parsing, or file-watching logic — all critical runtime paths are untested.",
    "CompletionAlertManager file polling — pollInterval of 1 second with FileManager.contentsOfDirectoryAtPath; no FSEvents or DispatchSource usage. Could miss events under heavy I/O."
  ],
  "test_observations": [
    "The project has ZERO test files and ZERO test targets. Package.swift declares only an executable target with no testTarget dependencies. The single TESTS edge in the graph refers to a production function testAlert (in AppState/CompletionAlertManager), not an actual unit test.",
    "Most critical untested logic: AppState.parseJson (JSON parsing with no error recovery), AppState.detectLatestThreads (SQLite queries + fallback), AppState.runShell/runCommand (Process spawning), ContextExporter.extract* methods (Python subprocess output parsing), and CompletionAlertManager state machine (event polling, sound playback, accessibility checks).",
    "ContextExporter is the best candidate for unit tests — all extract* methods and generateMarkdown are pure functions (take strings, return strings/arrays). Tests could use fixture JSONL files without needing a real Codex installation. generateMarkdown has multiple branches (empty messages, with/without function calls, with/without errors) that are completely uncovered.",
    "CompletionAlertManager's shouldAlert method and the minimumTaskDuration filtering logic are deterministic and could be tested with mock event files, but are currently untested.",
    "The AppState.Mode enum and its associated computed properties (modeTitle, modeSubtitle, modeColor, etc.) are pure logic that could be covered by simple unit tests.",
    "AdvancedCapabilities struct parsing and display helpers (WebSearchStatus, ImageInputInfo helpers, RoutingInfo.displayMode) are pure data transformations that are ideal for unit testing but have zero coverage.",
    "Existing functions named 'testAlert' are misnomers — they actually trigger an alert (production paths), not test it. This naming collision should be resolved to avoid confusion."
  ],
  "evidence": [
    "Architecture narrative in graph: 'The architecture has significant structural risks. Prioritize tech debt.' Risk level: high. 20 god components detected (ContentView 874 lines, ContinueWorkModal 675 lines, AppState 577 lines).",
    "Package.swift has no test target — only .executableTarget(name: 'DeepCodex', resources: [...]). No Tests/ directory exists in the file tree.",
    "Single TESTS edge (out of 523 total edges) connects to a production function named testAlert, not a unit test.",
    "Average cyclomatic complexity is 8.87, average cognitive complexity is 21.19 — high for a 10-file project.",
    "Hardcoded paths throughout: ~/.codex, ~/Developer/ai-tools/codex-mode/, /usr/bin/sqlite3, /usr/bin/python3, /usr/sbin/lsof, /System/Library/Sounds/, /Applications/Codex.app — these should be injectable for testability.",
    "detectLatestThreads runs raw SQL via string interpolation: \"...WHERE model_provider='\(provider)'...\" — a SQL injection vector from the provider string.",
    "MenuBarManager.Shell.run uses zsh -c with space-joined arguments: '\(path) \(args.joined(separator: \" \"))' — shell injection risk from mode names containing spaces or special characters."
  ],
  "summary": "DeepCodex is a macOS SwiftUI companion app (10 files, macOS 14+, no external dependencies) for managing Codex AI agent modes. It switches between GPT and DeepSeek models via a Moon Bridge proxy, exports portable context between sessions by parsing JSONL files with embedded Python scripts, and provides configurable completion alerts via Accessibility permissions. The architecture has serious structural risks: 6 god components (ContentView 874 lines, ContinueWorkModal 675 lines, AppState 577 lines, ContextExporter 396 lines, CompletionAlertManager 340 lines, MenuBarManager 169 lines), zero tests anywhere in the project, and high coupling centered on AppState (17+ dependents). The core data-access pattern (shelling out to sqlite3, python3, lsof, and bash) is untestable without refactoring toward dependency injection. The best candidate for immediate testing is ContextExporter, whose pure extraction and markdown-generation methods could be tested with static fixture files. The project also has two security concerns: SQL string interpolation in detectLatestThreads and shell command concatenation in MenuBarManager.Shell.run."
}
```

baseline.response:
Now I have a complete picture. Here is the onboarding summary:

```json
{
  "architecture": [
    "DeepCodex is a native macOS SwiftUI+AppKit app (Swift 6, macOS 14+) that controls the runtime mode of OpenAI's Codex desktop app. It acts as a mode switcher between 'Normal' (GPT-5.5/OpenAI), 'DeepSeek V4 Pro', and 'DeepSeek V4 Flash' (both via a Moon Bridge proxy).",
    "The app follows a single-window architecture with a central state object (AppState) that is observed by ContentView (SwiftUI). A menubar manager (MenuBarManager) provides secondary access and mode switching.",
    "State management is purely manual via @MainActor @StateObject + @Published properties. No Combine pipelines, no TCA, no redux pattern — direct mutation on the main actor.",
    "Communication with the outside world is entirely through shell subprocess calls (/bin/bash, /usr/sbin/lsof, /usr/bin/sqlite3, /usr/bin/python3, /usr/bin/find).",
    "The 'CompletionAlertManager' polls a JSON events directory on disk for new files matching 'agent-turn-complete-*.json', triggers audio alerts via AVAudioPlayer, and uses AX accessibility APIs to detect user interaction for auto-dismiss.",
    "The 'ContextExporter' module reads Codex session JSONL files via inline Python scripts, extracting messages, function calls, file paths, and errors to generate portable Markdown context for cross-mode continuation."
  ],
  "entry_points": [
    "Sources/DeepCodex/App.swift — @main struct DeepCodexApp: App with a renamed entry point (DeepCodex_main via build flag -Xfrontend -entry-point-function-name DeepCodex_main)",
    "Sources/DeepCodex/MenuBarManager.swift — setup() called from ContentView.onAppear, registers NSStatusItem with menu for mode switching without the window",
    "Sources/DeepCodex/AppState.swift — initialization via init() that kicks off Moon Bridge direct check and advanced capabilities loading"
  ],
  "important_modules": [
    "Sources/DeepCodex/AppState.swift (~400 lines) — Central ObservableObject: holds all app state, runs shell commands for mode switching, parses JSON status files, detects Codex threads via SQLite/fallback, manages alert settings persistence via UserDefaults",
    "Sources/DeepCodex/CompletionAlertManager.swift (~250 lines) — Polling-based file watcher for task-completion events, plays looping sounds (AVAudioPlayer with NSSound fallback), uses AXIsProcessTrusted for accessibility, monitors mouse/keyboard for auto-dismiss",
    "Sources/DeepCodex/ContextExporter.swift (~220 lines) — Read-only parser for Codex JSONL session files: uses embedded Python scripts to extract messages, tool calls, file paths, and errors, then generates structured Markdown context",
    "Sources/DeepCodex/ContentView.swift (~550 lines) — Main SwiftUI view with complex layout: header, action cards (3 mode buttons), collapsible tools panel, system info panel, and advanced capabilities section with web search/image input/routing cards",
    "Sources/DeepCodex/ContinueWorkModal.swift (~400 lines) — Multi-phase modal (loading → selecting → generating → ready/error) with thread grouping by project folder, destination mode picker, markdown preview, and copy/switch actions"
  ],
  "hotspots": [
    "AppState.swift — Shell command execution (runShell, runCommand) with blocking reads, JSON parsing, multiple async task chains, and a debug logging system; brittle parsing of status.sh output and Moon Bridge lsof output",
    "CompletionAlertManager.swift — Poll-based file watching (Timer at 1s interval), accessibility permission prompts, race conditions between stopTimer/resetTimer, AVAudioPlayer lifecycle, and focusBundleIds hardcoding",
    "ContextExporter.swift — Python scripts embedded as string literals in Swift code, heavy reliance on external python3/sqlite3 binaries with no error handling for missing tools, two fallback strategies for thread detection (SQLite JSON → filesystem find+grep)",
    "ContentView.swift — Large monolithic view (~550 lines) with nested conditional views, complex hover/active state animations, manual PreferenceKey-based height reporting for WindowSizer dynamic resize",
    "Thread detection (AppState.detectLatestThreads): SQLite query is provider-filtered but the schema depends on the Codex private state file (.codex/state_5.sqlite) which is undocumented and may change across Codex versions"
  ],
  "test_observations": [
    "There are ZERO tests in the entire project. Package.swift defines a single executable target with no test target, and no Tests/ directory exists anywhere in the repository.",
    "No XCTest, no Swift Testing framework, no Quick/Nimble — zero test infrastructure of any kind is present.",
    "The most critical untested code paths: all shell command execution/parsing (mode switching, Moon Bridge status, JSON file parsing), ContextExporter's Python-script-based JSONL parsing (fragile embedded scripts, no fallback testing), CompletionAlertManager's state machine (inactive → sounding → attended transitions, timer lifecycle), the thread detection SQLite query and its filesystem fallback, and the multiple async task chains in AppState with timing-dependent sleeps.",
    "The app depends entirely on side-effectful shell scripts (status.sh, codex-switch.sh, start-moonbridge.sh, advanced-capabilities-status.sh, restore-last-backup.sh) that live outside the repository (~/Developer/ai-tools/codex-mode/). These are not tested and are assumed to exist.",
    "AVAudioPlayer/NSSound fallback handling in CompletionAlertManager is untested, as is AXIsProcessTrusted accessibility permission management."
  ],
  "evidence": [
    "Package.swift defines a single executable target with no test target",
    "No Tests/ directory or *Test*.swift files exist anywhere in the project (confirmed via grep for 'import XCTest', 'import Testing', 'func test')",
    "All 9 source files were read in full: App.swift, AppState.swift, ContentView.swift, MenuBarManager.swift, CompletionAlertManager.swift, AdvancedCapabilities.swift, ContextExporter.swift, ContinueWorkModal.swift, Theme.swift",
    "Build artifacts confirm the compiled output is a macOS executable (DeepCodex) with 10 .o files (9 source + resource_bundle_accessor.swift)",
    "App icon and image resources exist: AppIcon.png, deepcodex_black.png, deepcodex_black_2.png"
  ],
  "summary": "DeepCodex is an un-tested macOS native SwiftUI app (~2,400 lines across 9 files) that serves as a mode-switching control panel for OpenAI's Codex desktop app. It lets users switch between Normal, DeepSeek V4 Pro, and DeepSeek V4 Flash modes by running shell scripts, monitors a Moon Bridge proxy, provides a file-polling-based completion alert system with accessibility-aware auto-dismiss, and includes a 'continue work' feature that parses Codex session files via embedded Python scripts to generate portable Markdown context. The codebase has zero tests, relies heavily on external shell scripts and binaries (sqlite3, python3, lsof, find), embeds Python as string literals, and uses polling-based file watching — all of which are high-risk untested areas. The architecture is straightforward: a single @MainActor AppState ObservableObject feeds a monolithic ContentView SwiftUI layout, with a menubar manager as a secondary UI channel. There are no external Swift dependencies, no CI configuration, and no documentation beyond the source code itself."
}
```
